title: Herbal Relief for Puffy Eyes: 10 Proven Remedies

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for puffy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fluid retention, which can cause puffiness around the eyes.

Kaempferol in Glycyrrrhhiza glabra is good for puffy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and improve blood flow, which can make the skin look smoother and less puffy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis decoction is a great remedy for puffy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties. When you use it, the antioxidants in calendula help to reduce swelling and soothe the skin around your eyes. This is especially helpful if you have puffy eyes due to allergies or irritations. The astringent properties in calendula also help to tighten the skin and reduce puffiness. Additionally, calendula has antiseptic properties, which help to prevent infection and speed up the healing process. When you apply the cooled decoction to your puffy eyes, the cooling sensation helps to reduce redness and ease the discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties of calendula also help to reduce the appearance of dark circles under the eyes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal decoction to help with puffy eyes, start by gathering the dried root of the licorice plant, also known as Glycyrrhiza glabra. Begin by measuring out a teaspoon of the dried root per cup of boiling water, then add it to a small saucepan. Pour in the boiling water and bring the mixture to a simmer for about 5-7 minutes. Allow the decoction to steep and the flavors to infuse, then strain it through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean bowl. Discard the solids and let the decoction cool to room temperature. Once cooled, dip a cotton pad into the decoction and gently apply it to your closed eyes, letting the liquid soak into the skin. Leave it on for a few minutes, then rinse your eyes with cold water and pat dry. This decoction is thought to help reduce puffiness and inflammation around the eyes due to its anti-inflammatory properties, making it a natural remedy to try.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis decoctions can help with puffy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties. When we're tired or eat salty foods, fluid can build up in our body and cause swelling in our eyes. The anti-inflammatory properties in Aloe barbadensis help to reduce this swelling by slowing down the release of chemicals that cause inflammation. This allows the swelling to go down and the eyes to feel less puffy. Aloe barbadensis also has soothing and calming effects on the skin, which can help to reduce redness and irritation around the eyes. Additionally, its moisturizing properties can help to hydrate the skin and reduce the appearance of dark circles, making the eyes look fresher and more rested.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a herbal decoction from Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, that helps with puffy eyes, start by gathering 1-2 tablespoons of dried licorice root. Rinse the root in cold water to remove any impurities, then chop it into small pieces. Next, place the chopped licorice root in a medium-sized pot and add 4 cups of water. Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. After the decoction has cooled slightly, strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Let the decoction cool to room temperature, then refrigerate it. To use, soak a cotton pad in the chilled decoction and apply it to your puffy eyes for 10-15 minutes. The anti-inflammatory properties of the licorice root may help reduce swelling and puffiness. Repeat this process as needed, but be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea decoctions can help reduce puffy eyes. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and redness in the skin. When you make a decoction from Echinacea purpurea roots, you release active compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid. These compounds can help calm down the skin and reduce inflammation. Puffy eyes often happen when there's excess fluid in the skin, and Echinacea purpurea decoctions can help reduce this fluid by improving blood circulation and lymphatic drainage. Additionally, its antioxidant properties can protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can also contribute to puffiness. By reducing inflammation and improving circulation, Echinacea purpurea decoctions can help make your eyes look less puffy and more relaxed.

You can use 1-2 teaspoons of the dried root for every cup of water. Place the licorice root in a saucepan with the desired amount of water and bring it to a boil. Once boiling, reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 5-10 minutes. The longer it cooks, the stronger the decoction will be. After it has cooled slightly, strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the solids. You can store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. To use, soak a cotton pad or a clean cloth in the decoction and gently apply it to your closed eyes. remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis decoctions are a natural remedy that can help with puffy eyes. The plant contains compounds that reduce swelling and inflammation, which are common causes of puffy eyes. When applied topically, these compounds can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid buildup. This can lead to a decrease in the appearance of puffiness and swelling. Additionally, Euphrasia officinalis decoctions have anti-irritant properties, which can soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes. This can help to reduce redness and irritation, making it easier to manage puffy eyes. To use Euphrasia officinalis decoctions for puffy eyes, simply soak a cotton pad in the solution and apply it to the affected area. Leave it on for a few minutes before rinsing off with cool water. This can be repeated as needed to help alleviate puffiness and promote a more refreshed and awake appearance.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal decoction that helps with puffy eyes, start by gathering 1-2 tablespoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root. You can purchase this at a local health food store or online. Rinse the root in cold water to remove any impurities, then place it in a small saucepan with 2 cups of water. Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. Next, remove the saucepan from the heat and let it steep for another 5-10 minutes to allow the root's active ingredients to infuse into the water. Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a bowl, and discard the solids. Allow the decoction to cool, then refrigerate it for later use. To use, dip a clean cotton pad or cloth into the decoction, wring it out thoroughly, and apply it to your closed eyes for 5-10 minutes. The tannins and flavonoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra are believed to help reduce puffiness and inflammation in the eyes, while also soothing and calming the skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, has been used for centuries to reduce puffiness around the eyes. The root contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which helps to decrease swelling. When Glycyrrhiza glabra is made into a decoction (a strong tea), it can be applied to the skin around the eyes using a cotton pad. The glycyrrhizin in the decoction reduces inflammation and water retention, which causes puffiness. As the decoction works, the skin around the eyes becomes less swollen, making it appear less puffy and tired. The decoction can also help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation. By using a Glycyrrhiza glabra decoction, you can reduce the appearance of puffy eyes and get a more rested and refreshed look.

Once cooled, soak a clean cotton pad or a cooled tea bag in the decoction and gently place it over your closed eyes for 5-10 minutes. The anti-inflammatory and soothing properties of licorice root can help reduce puffiness and leave your eyes feeling refreshed and calm.

intro_desc: Puffy eyes can be caused by fluid buildup, allergies, or lack of sleep. This can make you look tired and unhappy, affecting how you feel about yourself and your life. Using herbal decoctions can help fix the issue. One way to do this is by making a mixture with Calendula officinalis and Aloe barbadensis. Calendula officinalis has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling. Aloe barbadensis is known for its soothing and calming effects, which can help relax the skin and reduce puffiness. When applied to the skin, these herbs can help reduce the appearance of puffy eyes and leave you looking more refreshed and alert.  By fixing this issue, you can improve your life in many ways. When you feel good about how you look, you're more likely to feel confident and happy. This can have a positive impact on your relationships and daily activities. You may find that you're more motivated to take care of yourself and your appearance, which can lead to a healthier and more balanced lifestyle. Additionally, taking care of your skin and eyes can help you feel more rested and alert, which can improve your productivity and overall well-being.  To make a herbal decoction for puffy eyes, you can steep Calendula officinalis and Aloe barbadensis in hot water. Let it cool down, then soak a cotton pad in the mixture and apply it to your eyes. You can also add a few drops of the mixture to a cold compress and apply it to your eyes for a few minutes.
supplementary_best: To remedy puffy eyes, a combination of Hamamelis virginiana, Aloe barbadensis, and Euphrasia officinalis decoctions is effective. Hamamelis virginiana, also known as witch hazel, is a natural anti-inflammatory and astringent that helps reduce swelling and calm irritated skin. Aloe barbadensis, or aloe vera, is a soothing and moisturizing herb that cools the skin and reduces redness. Euphrasia officinalis, or eye bright, is a herb traditionally used to treat eye problems, including puffiness, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and improve circulation. This combination is the best because it addresses multiple aspects of puffy eyes: reducing inflammation (Hamamelis virginiana), soothing and moisturizing the skin (Aloe barbadensis), and improving circulation (Euphrasia officinalis). The anti-inflammatory and astringent properties of Hamamelis virginiana work well with the soothing properties of Aloe barbadensis to calm and cool the skin, while Euphrasia officinalis helps improve circulation to reduce puffiness. This combination provides a comprehensive remedy for puffy eyes.
supplementary_avoid: If you have puffy eyes, it's best to avoid using herbal decoctions that can irritate or inflame the skin around your eyes. One such herb is 'Capsicum annuum', which contains capsaicin, a compound that can cause burning and itching sensations. This can make puffiness worse and cause further irritation. Another herb to avoid is 'Sambucus nigra', as its berries can cause allergic reactions and skin inflammation. 'Rhus toxicodendron' is also not recommended, as it can cause contact dermatitis, a skin condition characterized by redness, swelling, and itching. 'Ephedra sinica' and 'Yohimbe bark' are also best avoided, as they can increase heart rate and blood pressure, which can worsen puffiness by causing fluid retention. Finally, 'Ginkgo biloba' and 'Cinchona bark' should be used with caution, as they can thin the blood and cause bruising or bleeding under the eyes.
supplementary_other_remedies: Cold compresses can help reduce puffy eyes by constricting blood vessels and reducing swelling. When a cold compress is applied to the affected area, the blood vessels underneath the eyes constrict, which in turn reduces the flow of blood and the amount of fluid that accumulates, making the eyes appear less puffy.  Cucumber slices are another natural remedy that can help alleviate puffy eyes. The cool temperature of the cucumber can help constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling, while its anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce redness and puffiness.  Eye creams or serums rich in caffeine can also help alleviate puffy eyes. Caffeine has a constricting effect on blood vessels, which can help to reduce puffiness and make the eyes appear less swollen.  Aromatherapy using essential oils like peppermint, chamomile, or eucalyptus may also help to alleviate puffy eyes. Inhaling the scent of these oils can help to reduce swelling and relax the muscles around the eyes, which can help to reduce puffiness. However, it's essential to use these oils in moderation and in combination with other remedies, as excessive use can irritate the skin around the eyes.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have puffy eyes may also experience other ailments like sinus pressure, headaches, and fatigue. This is because puffiness in the eyes is often a sign of water retention, which can be caused by allergies, colds, or digestive issues. Medicinal herbs like ginger, turmeric, and peppermint can help alleviate these symptoms. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce sinus pressure and ease congestion. Turmeric contains curcumin, which has antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and fight off infections. Peppermint oil has a natural decongestant effect that can help ease sinus pressure and headaches.  Additionally, people with puffy eyes may also experience bloating and digestive issues, as water retention can be a symptom of digestive problems. Herbs like dandelion root, licorice root, and chamomile can help with digestion and reduce water retention. Dandelion root has diuretic properties that can help flush out excess fluids and reduce bloating. Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation. Chamomile has a calming effect that can help relax the muscles and reduce water retention.  Some people with puffy eyes may also experience dark circles and eye strain due to poor circulation and fatigue. Herbs like rosemary, ginseng, and bilberry can help improve circulation and reduce fatigue. Rosemary has antioxidant properties that can help improve circulation and reduce oxidative stress. Ginseng has adaptogenic properties that can help reduce fatigue and improve energy levels. Bilberry has anthocyanins, which can help improve circulation and reduce eye strain.  Lastly, people with puffy eyes may also experience skin issues like eczema and acne due to inflammation and water retention. Herbs like calendula, aloe vera, and tea tree oil can help soothe and calm the skin. Calendula has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe eczema and reduce inflammation. Aloe vera has soothing properties that can help calm acne and reduce inflammation. Tea tree oil has antibacterial properties that can help fight off acne-causing bacteria.
